patch_description: Inspired by prior attempts to diversify hub entangling axes while keeping a compact parameter budget, this block adopts global (shared) ring entanglement via CRX around the board perimeter and a shared hub entanglement via CRY from the center to each edge. This reduces parameter count per block (11 keys vs 13 in the previous variant) and leverages structured symmetry: shared pre/post-RZ and shared RY layers preserve Tic-Tac-Toe board symmetries while the ring/hub interactions provide richer inter-qubit correlations without exploding the parameter budget. This mirrors the second program’s improvements (0.60 combined score) by collapsing ring and hub gates to single shared parameters, potentially improving generalization and convergence with Adam.
